pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 29 confirms '8 appliances' in CSV, and manual count of CSV data rows (Refrigerator, HVAC, Washing Machine, Dryer, Dishwasher, Water Heater, Microwave, Garbage Disposal)

Task claims $0.12/kWh from setup_task.sh, but current average US residential electricity rate is $0.1724/kWh (March 2026 per Choose Energy). Rate appears historically accurate (closer to 2020-2022 levels around $0.12-0.13) but is below current 2024+ rates of $0.1648-0.1724/kWh.
Task claims 14 years. Web search indicates refrigerator lifespans range 10-15 years according to multiple sources including Mr. Appliance and InterNACHI. Task value is within verified range.
Task claims 15 years. Web search confirms HVAC systems last 15-20 years. Task value matches low end of range.
Task claims 11 years. Web search indicates 5-7 years typical for new models, but up to 10-13 years with good maintenance. Task value is within reasonable range.
Task claims $1200. Web search indicates typical range $600-2300 for new refrigerators. Task value is within verified range.
Task claims $1100. Web search confirms average tank water heater cost is $1100 (exact match).
Task claims $700. Web search indicates typical dryer replacement cost is $800-1200. Task value is slightly below range but reasonable.
Task claims 600 kWh/year. Web search indicates average refrigerator uses ~657 kWh/year. Task value is very close and reasonable.
Task claims 3500 kWh/year. Web search indicates HVAC systems use more than 2000 kWh/year (more than half of avg home usage). Task value is reasonable estimate within expected range.

Task claims 4500 kWh/year. Web search indicates water heating is 14-18% of household energy use. For ~15,000 kWh/year typical home, this would be ~2100-2700 kWh. Task value of 4500 seems high but not impossible for larger households.

pi_items.metadata_value: Industry standard: don't repair if cost exceeds 50% of replacement cost
pi_items.source: Task description and verifier implement the '50% rule'. Web search confirms this is indeed an established industry standard for appliance repair decisions. Multiple sources (Standard TV & Appliance, House Digest, etc.) confirm: if repair cost > 50% of replacement cost, replace instead.
setup_task.sh creates CSV data inline (lines 13-23) rather than downloading from external source. Data contains realistic but fictional appliance inventory for teaching purposes.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic appliance lifecycle data created for educational purposes. The data includes 8 realistic appliances with verified parameters: refrigerator lifespan of 14 years (within 10-15 industry standard), HVAC 15 years (within 15-20 range), typical replacement costs ($1100 for water heater matches industry averages, $1200 for refrigerator is within $600-2300 range). Energy consumption estimates are reasonable (refrigerator 600 kWh/year vs. 657 kWh/year average, HVAC 3500 kWh/year exceeds typical >2000 kWh/year baseline). However, the electricity rate of $0.12/kWh is below current 2024-2026 US averages of $0.165-0.172/kWh, reflecting older rate assumptions. The task correctly implements the verified '50% repair rule' industry standard. No real external datasets or privileged information sources are involved.
